_comment: Signature library for tools/hooks/tooling_error_guard.py (PostToolUse). Each signature recognizes a KNOWN, permanently-fixable tooling/shell/environment failure. tier 'block' writes an incident marker (.bb2_tooling_incident.json) + blocks the turn until resolved; tier 'warn' only prints a stderr hint. MATCHING: a signature fires if ANY of its 'substrings' (matched literally, case-insensitive) OR 'regexes' (Python re, case-insensitive) appear in the tool output. ADDING A SIGNATURE HERE IS ITSELF A VALID PERMANENT FIX -- when a new failure mode is discovered, encode it so the next agent gets instant, specific guidance. Keep signatures HIGH-PRECISION (they fire on every Bash/PowerShell result); a false positive that blocks legitimate work is worse than a miss. See docs/tooling_incidents.md for the running ledger.

build_file._comment: Files that MUST use LF line endings (the GNU toolchain and bash break on CRLF). Used by the post-write CRLF check on Edit/Write/MultiEdit. A path is build-critical if its lowercased suffix is in 'suffixes', its basename is in 'names', or its basename matches a glob in 'name_globs'. Mirrors AGENTS.md 'File-edit conventions'.

signatures.summary: A shell script/command was parsed with Windows CRLF line endings -- bash choked on a trailing carriage return (\r).
signatures.root_cause: A .sh / .py / build file was saved with CRLF (\r\n) instead of LF. Usually a Windows-side editor or the Edit/Write tool introduced it silently. See memory rules crlf-via-edit-write and file-editing-wsl.
permanent_fix: Normalize the offending file to LF -- in WSL/Bash:  sed -i 's/\r$//' <file>   (or  perl -pi -e 's/\r//g' <file> ).
Prevent recurrence: confirm the file's pattern is covered by .gitattributes 'eol=lf', and edit build files via WSL/Bash (heredoc) rather than the Windows Edit/Write tool.
If a NEW path pattern was involved, add it to .gitattributes AND to build_file in this signatures file so the live CRLF check catches it next time.
signatures.suggested_guard: .gitattributes eol=lf entry covering the path + the post-write CRLF check in tooling_error_guard.py

signatures.summary: An Edit/Write/MultiEdit just wrote CRLF line endings into a build-critical file (LF is required).
signatures.root_cause: The Windows Edit/Write tool converts LF->CRLF silently when writing some files (memory: crlf-via-edit-write). The GNU toolchain / bash then break on the carriage returns.
permanent_fix: Normalize the file you just wrote back to LF -- in WSL/Bash:  sed -i 's/\r$//' <file> .
Re-make the affected target to confirm the build still matches (CRLF in a pipeline .txt or src/*.c silently corrupts codegen).
Prevent recurrence: ensure .gitattributes has an 'eol=lf' entry for this path; for the LF-critical pipeline .txt files, prefer editing via WSL rather than Edit/Write.

substrings: There is no distribution with the supplied name
Windows Subsystem for Linux has no installed distributions
signatures.summary: WSL is unavailable, not on PATH, or has no running distribution -- the Linux toolchain can't be reached.
signatures.root_cause: WSL isn't installed/started, the default distro is wrong/stopped, or 'wsl' isn't on PATH for this shell.
permanent_fix: Confirm the distro: in PowerShell  wsl -l -v ; start it / set default with  wsl --set-default <distro> .
Make the project's WSL entrypoint robust: tools/wsl.sh and tools/dc.sh should detect a missing/stopped distro and emit ONE actionable line (not a raw failure). Add that detection if absent.
Document the one-time setup step in AGENTS.md (build commands) so a fresh machine never hits this blind.
signatures.suggested_guard: A preflight check in tools/wsl.sh that verifies 'wsl -l -q' lists a running distro before invoking, with a clear fix message

signatures.summary: A gitignored build dependency (cc1 / maspsx / .venv / disc / permuter) is missing or its worktree symlink is broken.
signatures.root_cause: This worktree's deps were never linked, the bootstrap didn't run, or a symlink into the main checkout went stale. Worktrees do NOT carry their own copies of these gitignored deps -- they are symlinked from the main checkout by the bootstrap.
permanent_fix: Re-link the deps:  bash tools/dc.sh bootstrap   (idempotent; links .venv, gcc-2.7.2, decomp-permuter, m2c, disc from the main checkout).
Confirm:  bash tools/dc.sh verify-toolchain   (should print 'Toolchain: OK').

Prevent recurrence: if bootstrap did NOT relink it, fix tools/worktree_bootstrap.sh so it detects a STALE/DANGLING symlink (not just a missing one) and re-creates it. A symlink that exists but points nowhere is the usual silent trap.
signatures.suggested_guard: Make tools/worktree_bootstrap.sh re-link dangling symlinks, and have tools/wsl.sh auto-bootstrap when a required dep is missing (it already does this for cc1 -- extend to the others)

substrings: Too many levels of symbolic links
signatures.summary: A symlink is circular or points through a broken chain -- almost always a worktree dep-link gone wrong.
signatures.root_cause: A bootstrap symlink was created pointing at itself or at another symlink that points back (e.g. re-bootstrapping a worktree whose target moved).
permanent_fix: Remove the bad link and re-link:  in the worktree,  rm <link> && bash tools/dc.sh bootstrap .
Prevent recurrence: tools/worktree_bootstrap.sh must use an absolute target to the MAIN checkout and refuse to link a path onto itself. Add that guard if missing.
signatures.suggested_guard: A self-link / absolute-target assertion in tools/worktree_bootstrap.sh

signatures.summary: A core build tool (make / python3 / maspsx) is not on PATH for this shell.
signatures.root_cause: Wrong shell (Git Bash vs WSL), the .venv isn't activated, or the tool genuinely isn't installed in this environment.
permanent_fix: Route build/pipeline commands through WSL where the toolchain lives:  bash tools/wsl.sh '<cmd>'  (auto-detects cwd).
If python3 is missing only in .venv context, activate it (source .venv/bin/activate) or run  bash tools/dc.sh setup-venv .
Prevent recurrence: if a command was run in the wrong shell, fix the caller (script/alias) to invoke via tools/wsl.sh, not raw 'make'/'python3'.
signatures.suggested_guard: Callers that need the Linux toolchain should go through tools/wsl.sh; add a PATH check at the top of any new build script
